--- Step 26 ---
🎬 Tool call: Write({'path': 'solution.py', 'content': 'MOD = 998244353\n\nS = input().strip()\nn = len(S)\n\n# Track per-letter DP but optimize transitions using aggregates.\n# dp00[d]: count with double of letter d, no lowercase after\n# dp01[d]: count with double of letter d, lowercase after\n#\n# Key insight for \'?\' transitions:\n# - When distributing uppercase to "other letters", use sum instead of looping\n# - s00 = sum(dp00), s01 = sum(dp01)\n\ndp00 = [0] * 26  # per-letter: double, no_lower\ndp01 = [0] * 26  # per-letter: double, has_lower\ndp0 = 1  # no double yet\n\nfor c in S:\n    if c == \'?\':\n        # Transitions for \'?\':\n        # From dp0:\n        #   - 26 lowercase: stay dp0\n        #   - 26 uppercase: each letter d gets +dp0 (becomes double d)\n        # From dp00[d]:\n        #   - 1 uppercase == d: stays dp00[d]\n        #   - 25 uppercase != d: goes to dp00[x] for x != d\n        #   - 26 lowercase: goes to dp01[d]\n        # From dp01[d]:\n        #   - 1 uppercase == d: stays dp01[d]\n        #   - 25 uppercase != d: DDoS (discard)\n        #   - 26 lowercase: stays dp01[d]\n        \n        s00 = sum(dp00) % MOD\n        s01 = sum(dp01) % MOD\n        \n        new_dp0 = (dp0 * 26) % MOD\n        \n        # Compute new dp00 and dp01\n        # For each letter d:\n        # new_dp00[d] = dp0 + dp00[d] + (s00 - dp00[d]) = dp0 + s00\n        # new_dp01[d] = dp01[d] * 26 + dp01[d] + dp01[d] * 26 = dp01[d] * 53 ... wait that\'s wrong\n        \n        # Let me redo:\n        # From dp0: +26 dp00 entries each get +dp0\n        # From dp00:\n        #   dp00[d] contributes:\n        #     - 1 to new_dp00[d] (same letter stays)\n        #     - 1 to each new_dp00[x] for x != d (25 total)\n        #     - 26 to new_dp01[d] (lowercase)\n        # From dp01:\n        #   dp01[d] contributes:\n        #     - 1 to new_dp01[d] (same letter uppercase)\n        #     - 26 to new_dp01[d] (lowercase)\n        \n        # new_dp00[d] = dp0 + sum over x != d of dp00[x] + dp00[d] * 1\n        #             = dp0 + (s00 - dp00[d]) + dp00[d]\n        #             = dp0 + s00\n        # So all new_dp00[d] are equal! They\'re all (dp0 + s00) % MOD\n        \n        # new_dp01[d] = dp01[d] * 26 (lowercase) + dp01[d] * 1 (same uppercase)\n        #             = dp01[d] * 27\n        # But also dp00[d] contributes 26 to new_dp01[d]:\n        # new_dp01[d] = dp01[d] * 27 + dp00[d] * 26\n        \n        new_dp00_val = (dp0 + s00) % MOD\n        new_dp00 = [new_dp00_val] * 26\n        \n        new_dp01 = []\n        for d in range(26):\n            new_dp01.append((dp01[d] * 27 + dp00[d] * 26) % MOD)\n        \n        dp0 = new_dp0\n        dp00 = new_dp00\n        dp01 = new_dp01\n        \n    elif c.isupper():\n        c_idx = ord(c) - ord(\'A\')\n        \n        # Transitions for specific uppercase letter:\n        # From dp0: goes to dp00[c_idx]\n        # From dp00:\n        #   - if d == c_idx: stays dp00[d]\n        #   - if d != c_idx: goes to dp00[c_idx]\n        # From dp01:\n        #   - if d == c_idx: stays dp01[d]\n        #   - if d != c_idx: DDoS (discard)\n        \n        s00 = sum(dp00) % MOD\n        s01 = sum(dp01) % MOD\n        \n        # new_dp0[c_idx] = dp0 + sum over d != c_idx of dp00[d]\n        #                = dp0 + (s00 - dp00[c_idx])\n        # new_dp00[d] for d != c_idx: stays dp00[d]\n        \n        # new_dp01[c_idx] = dp01[c_idx] (stays)\n        # new_dp01[d] for d != c_idx: 0 (DDoS)\n        \n        new_dp00 = [0] * 26\n        for d in range(26):\n            if d == c_idx:\n                new_dp00[d] = (dp0 + s00 - dp00[d]) % MOD\n            else:\n                new_dp00[d] = dp00[d]\n        \n        new_dp01 = [0] * 26\n        new_dp01[c_idx] = dp01[c_idx]\n        \n        dp00 = new_dp00\n        dp01 = new_dp01\n        \n    else:  # c is lowercase\n        # Transitions for lowercase:\n        # From dp0: stay dp0\n        # From dp00[d]: goes to dp01[d]\n        # From dp01[d]: stays dp01[d]\n        \n        new_dp0 = dp0\n        \n        new_dp00 = [0] * 26  # all go to dp01\n        new_dp01 = [0] * 26\n        for d in range(26):\n            new_dp01[d] = (dp00[d] + dp01[d]) % MOD\n        \n        dp0 = new_dp0\n        dp00 = new_dp00\n        dp01 = new_dp01\n\n# Answer: sum of dp00 (double exists, no lowercase after)\nresult = sum(dp00) % MOD\nprint(result)\n'})

👀 Observation: File: problem.txt

Metadata: file_mtime_ms=1774774829702, file_size_bytes=1880, expected_mtime_ms=1774774829702, expected_size_bytes=1880

 1 | Title: Prerequisites
 2 | Platform: atcoder
 3 | Difficulty: hard
 4 | Contest date: 2023-08-19T00:00:00
 5 | Mode: stdin
 6 | 
 7 | We have N books numbered 1 to N.
 8 | Book i assumes that you have read C_i books, the j-th of which is book P_{i,j}: you must read all these C_i books before reading book i.
 9 | Here, you can read all the books in some order.
10 | You are trying to read the minimum number of books required to read book 1.
11 | Print the numbers of the books you must read excluding book 1 in the order they should be read. Under this condition, the set of books to read is uniquely determined.
12 | If there are multiple reading orders that satisfy the condition, you may print any of them.
13 | 
14 | Input
15 | 
16 | The input is given from Standard Input in the following format:
17 | N
18 | C_1 P_{1,1} \ldots P_{1,C_1}
19 | C_2 P_{2,1} \ldots P_{2,C_2}
20 | \vdots
21 | C_N P_{N,1} \ldots P_{N,C_N}
22 | 
23 | Output
24 | 
25 | Print the numbers of the books you must read to read book 1 in the order they should be read, with spaces in between.
26 | 
27 | Constraints
28 | 
29 | 
30 | - 2 \leq N \leq 2 \times 10^5
31 | - 0 \leq C_i < N
32 | - \sum_{i=1}^{N} C_i \leq 2 \times 10^5
33 | - C_1 \geq 1
34 | - 1 \leq P_{i,j} \leq N
35 | - P_{i,j} \neq P_{i,k} for 1 \leq j < k \leq C_i.
36 | - It is possible to read all the books.
37 | 
38 | Sample Input 1
39 | 
40 | 6
41 | 3 2 3 4
42 | 2 3 5
43 | 0
44 | 1 5
45 | 0
46 | 0
47 | 
48 | Sample Output 1
49 | 
50 | 5 3 4 2
51 | 
52 | To read book 1, you must read books 2,3,4; to read book 2, you must read books 3,5; to read book 4, you must read book 5. To read books 3,5,6, you do not have to read any other books.
53 | For example, if you read books 5,3,4,2 in this order, you can read book 1. This is a correct answer, because you will never be able to read book 1 with three or fewer books read. As another example, reading books 3,5,4,2 in this order also allows you to read book 1 with 4 books read.
54 |

63 | 0

--- Step 28 ---
🎬 Tool call: Write({'path': 'solution.py', 'content': 'import sys\nimport heapq\n\ndef solve():\n    input_data = sys.stdin.read().split()\n    idx = 0\n    N = int(input_data[idx]); idx += 1\n    \n    # adj[v] = list of books that v requires (v -> u means v requires u)\n    adj = [[] for _ in range(N + 1)]\n    for i in range(1, N + 1):\n        C = int(input_data[idx]); idx += 1\n        for _ in range(C):\n            p = int(input_data[idx]); idx += 1\n            adj[i].append(p)\n    \n    # Find all reachable nodes from book 1 (prerequisites of book 1)\n    # Using iterative DFS\n    visited_nodes = set()\n    stack = [1]\n    while stack:\n        v = stack.pop()\n        if v in visited_nodes:\n            continue\n        visited_nodes.add(v)\n        for u in adj[v]:\n            if u not in visited_nodes:\n                stack.append(u)\n    \n    # Remove book 1 from visited_nodes (we don\'t output it)\n    visited_nodes.remove(1)\n    \n    # Topological sort using max-heap (largest index first)\n    # Build reverse adjacency: for each book, which books require it\n    reverse_adj = {v: [] for v in visited_nodes}\n    \n    for v in visited_nodes:\n        for u in adj[v]:\n            if u in visited_nodes:\n                reverse_adj[u].append(v)  # v requires u, so u comes before v\n    \n    # Compute in_degree for topological sort\n    in_degree = {v: 0 for v in visited_nodes}\n    for v in visited_nodes:\n        for u in adj[v]:\n            if u in visited_nodes:\n                in_degree[v] += 1\n    \n    # Find all books with in_degree 0 (no prerequisites within our set)\n    # Use max-heap (negative for max-heap behavior)\n    heap = []\n    for v in visited_nodes:\n        if in_degree[v] == 0:\n            heapq.heappush(heap, -v)\n    \n    result = []\n    while heap:\n        # Pop the largest available book\n        v = -heapq.heappop(heap)\n        result.append(v)\n        \n        # For each book that requires v, decrease its in_degree\n        for req_book in reverse_adj[v]:\n            in_degree[req_book] -= 1\n            if in_degree[req_book] == 0:\n                heapq.heappush(heap, -req_book)\n    \n    # Reverse to get the correct topological order\n    print(\' \'.join(map(str, result[::-1])))\n\nif __name__ == "__main__":\n    solve()\n'})

--- Step 29 ---
🎬 Tool call: Write({'path': 'solution.py', 'content': "MOD = 998244353\n\nS = input().strip()\n\n# dp0: count of ways with no double yet\n# dp00[d]: count with double of letter d, no lowercase after\n# dp01[d]: count with double of letter d, lowercase after\n\ndp0 = 1  # start: no double yet\ndp00 = [0] * 26  # per-letter: double, no_lower\ndp01 = [0] * 26  # per-letter: double, has_lower\n\nfor c in S:\n    if c == '?':\n        # Store old values for simultaneous update\n        old_dp0 = dp0\n        old_dp00 = dp00[:]\n        old_dp01 = dp01[:]\n        \n        s00 = sum(old_dp00) % MOD\n        \n        new_dp0 = (old_dp0 * 26) % MOD\n        \n        # new_dp00[d] = old_dp0 + s00 (all letters become symmetric)\n        new_dp00_val = (old_dp0 + s00) % MOD\n        new_dp00 = [new_dp00_val] * 26\n        \n        # new_dp01[d] = old_dp01[d] * 27 + old_dp00[d] * 26\n        new_dp01 = [(old_dp01[d] * 27 + old_dp00[d] * 26) % MOD for d in range(26)]\n        \n        dp0 = new_dp0\n        dp00 = new_dp00\n        dp01 = new_dp01\n        \n    elif c.isupper():\n        c_idx = ord(c) - ord('A')\n        \n        # Store old values\n        old_dp0 = dp0\n        old_dp00 = dp00[:]\n        old_dp01 = dp01[:]\n        \n        s00 = sum(old_dp00) % MOD\n        \n        # new_dp0[c_idx] = old_dp0 + sum over d != c_idx of old_dp00[d]\n        #                 = old_dp0 + (s00 - old_dp00[c_idx])\n        # new_dp00[d] for d != c_idx: stays old_dp00[d]\n        new_dp00 = [0] * 26\n        for d in range(26):\n            if d == c_idx:\n                new_dp00[d] = (old_dp0 + s00 - old_dp00[d]) % MOD\n            else:\n                new_dp00[d] = old_dp00[d]\n        \n        # new_dp01[c_idx] = old_dp01[c_idx] (stays, other dp01 values go to DDoS)\n        new_dp01 = [0] * 26\n        new_dp01[c_idx] = old_dp01[c_idx]\n        \n        dp00 = new_dp00\n        dp01 = new_dp01\n        \n    else:  # c is lowercase\n        # From dp0: stay dp0\n        # From dp00[d]: goes to dp01[d]\n        # From dp01[d]: stays dp01[d]\n        \n        new_dp01 = [(dp00[d] + dp01[d]) % MOD for d in range(26)]\n        dp0 = dp0\n        dp00 = [0] * 26\n        dp01 = new_dp01\n\n# Answer: sum of dp00 (double exists, no lowercase after)\nresult = sum(dp00) % MOD\nprint(result)\n"})

--- Step 58 ---
🎬 Tool call: Write({'path': 'solution.py', 'content': 'import sys\nimport heapq\n\ndef solve():\n    input_data = sys.stdin.read().split()\n    idx = 0\n    N = int(input_data[idx]); idx += 1\n    \n    # adj[v] = list of books that v requires (v -> u means v requires u)\n    adj = [[] for _ in range(N + 1)]\n    for i in range(1, N + 1):\n        C = int(input_data[idx]); idx += 1\n        for _ in range(C):\n            p = int(input_data[idx]); idx += 1\n            adj[i].append(p)\n    \n    # Find all reachable nodes from book 1 (prerequisites of book 1)\n    # Using iterative DFS\n    visited_nodes = set()\n    stack = [1]\n    while stack:\n        v = stack.pop()\n        if v in visited_nodes:\n            continue\n        visited_nodes.add(v)\n        for u in adj[v]:\n            if u not in visited_nodes:\n                stack.append(u)\n    \n    # Remove book 1 from visited_nodes (we don\'t output it)\n    visited_nodes.remove(1)\n    \n    # Topological sort using max-heap (largest index first)\n    # Build reverse adjacency: for each book, which books require it\n    reverse_adj = {v: [] for v in visited_nodes}\n    \n    for v in visited_nodes:\n        for u in adj[v]:\n            if u in visited_nodes:\n                reverse_adj[u].append(v)  # v requires u, so u comes before v\n    \n    # Compute in_degree for topological sort\n    in_degree = {v: 0 for v in visited_nodes}\n    for v in visited_nodes:\n        for u in adj[v]:\n            if u in visited_nodes:\n                in_degree[v] += 1\n    \n    # Find all books with in_degree 0 (no prerequisites within our set)\n    # Use max-heap (negative for max-heap behavior)\n    heap = []\n    for v in visited_nodes:\n        if in_degree[v] == 0:\n            heapq.heappush(heap, -v)\n    \n    result = []\n    while heap:\n        # Pop the largest available book\n        v = -heapq.heappop(heap)\n        result.append(v)\n        \n        # For each book that requires v, decrease its in_degree\n        for req_book in reverse_adj[v]:\n            in_degree[req_book] -= 1\n            if in_degree[req_book] == 0:\n                heapq.heappush(heap, -req_book)\n    \n    # Output directly (not reversed)\n    print(\' \'.join(map(str, result)))\n\nif __name__ == "__main__":\n    solve()\n'})

--- Step 31 ---
🎬 Tool call: Write({'path': 'solution.py', 'content': "MOD = 998244353\n\nS = input().strip()\n\n# dp0: count of ways with no double yet\n# dp00[d]: count with double of letter d, no lowercase after\n# dp01[d]: count with double of letter d, lowercase after\n\ndp0 = 1  # start: no double yet\ndp00 = [0] * 26  # per-letter: double, no_lower\ndp01 = [0] * 26  # per-letter: double, has_lower\n\nfor c in S:\n    if c == '?':\n        # Store old values for simultaneous update\n        old_dp0 = dp0\n        old_dp00 = dp00[:]\n        old_dp01 = dp01[:]\n        \n        s00 = sum(old_dp00) % MOD\n        \n        new_dp0 = (old_dp0 * 26) % MOD\n        \n        # new_dp00[d] = old_dp0 + s00 (all letters become symmetric)\n        new_dp00_val = (old_dp0 + s00) % MOD\n        new_dp00 = [new_dp00_val] * 26\n        \n        # new_dp01[d] = old_dp01[d] * 27 + old_dp00[d] * 26\n        new_dp01 = [(old_dp01[d] * 27 + old_dp00[d] * 26) % MOD for d in range(26)]\n        \n        dp0 = new_dp0\n        dp00 = new_dp00\n        dp01 = new_dp01\n        \n    elif c.isupper():\n        c_idx = ord(c) - ord('A')\n        \n        # Store old values\n        old_dp0 = dp0\n        old_dp00 = dp00[:]\n        old_dp01 = dp01[:]\n        \n        s00 = sum(old_dp00) % MOD\n        \n        # new_dp0[c_idx] = old_dp0 + s00 (all dp00 goes to c_idx when we see c)\n        # new_dp00[d] for d != c_idx: stays old_dp00[d]\n        new_dp00 = [0] * 26\n        new_dp00[c_idx] = (old_dp0 + s00) % MOD\n        for d in range(26):\n            if d != c_idx:\n                new_dp00[d] = old_dp00[d]\n        \n        # new_dp01[c_idx] = old_dp01[c_idx] (stays, other dp01 values go to DDoS)\n        new_dp01 = [0] * 26\n        new_dp01[c_idx] = old_dp01[c_idx]\n        \n        dp00 = new_dp00\n        dp01 = new_dp01\n        \n    else:  # c is lowercase\n        # From dp0: stay dp0\n        # From dp00[d]: goes to dp01[d]\n        # From dp01[d]: stays dp01[d]\n        \n        new_dp01 = [(dp00[d] + dp01[d]) % MOD for d in range(26)]\n        dp0 = dp0\n        dp00 = [0] * 26\n        dp01 = new_dp01\n\n# Answer: sum of dp00 (double exists, no lowercase after)\nresult = sum(dp00) % MOD\nprint(result)\n"})

adj[v] = list of books that v requires (v -> u means v requires u)\n    adj = [[] for _ in range(N + 1)]\n    for i in range(1, N + 1):\n        C = int(input_data[idx]); idx += 1\n        for _ in range(C):\n            p = int(input_data[idx]); idx += 1\n            adj[i].append(p)\n    \n    # Find all reachable nodes from book 1 (prerequisites of book 1)\n    # Using iterative DFS\n    visited_nodes = set()\n    stack = [1]\n    while stack:\n        v = stack.pop()\n        if v in visited_nodes:\n            continue\n        visited_nodes.add(v)\n        for u in adj[v]:\n            if u not in visited_nodes:\n                stack.append(u)\n    \n    # Remove book 1 from visited_nodes (we don\'t output it)\n    visited_nodes.remove(1)\n    \n    # Sort adj for each node (ascending) for consistent behavior\n    for v in adj:\n        adj[v].sort()\n    \n    # Topological sort using Kahn\'s algorithm\n    # Compute in_degree\n    in_degree = {v: 0 for v in visited_nodes}\n    \n    for v in visited_nodes:\n        for u in adj[v]:\n            if u in visited_nodes:\n                in_degree[v] += 1\n    \n    # 🎬 Tool call: Write({'path': 'solution.py', 'content': 'import sys\nsys.setrecursionlimit(500000)\nimport heapq\n\ndef solve():\n    input_data = sys.stdin.read().split()\n    idx = 0\n    N = int(input_data[idx]); idx += 1\n            adj[i].append(p)\n    \n    # Find all reachable nodes from book 1 (prerequisites of book 1)\n    # Using iterative DFS\n    visited_nodes = set()\n    stack = [1]\n    while stack:\n        v = stack.pop()\n        if v in visited_nodes:\n            continue\n        visited_nodes.add(v)\n        for u in adj[v]:\n            if u not in visited_nodes:\n                stack.append(u)\n    \n    # Remove book 1 from visited_nodes (we don\'t output it)\n    visited_nodes.remove(1)\n    \n    # Topological sort using max-heap (largest index first)\n    # Compute in_degree\n    in_degree = {v: 0 for v in visited_nodes}\n    \n    for v in visited_nodes:\n        for u in adj[v]:\n            if u in visited_nodes:\n                in_degree[v] += 1\n    \n    # Find all books with in_degree 0\n    # Use max-heap (negative for max-heap behavior)\n    heap = [-v for v in visited_nodes if in_degree[v] == 0]\n    heapq.heapify(heap)\n    result = []\n    result_set = set()\n    \n    while heap:\n        # Pop the largest available (negative)\n        v = -heapq.heappop(heap)\n        result.append(v)\n        result_set.add(v)\n        \n        # Decrease in_degree for books that require v\n        for other in visited_nodes:\n            if other in result_set:\n                continue\n            if v in adj[other]:\n                in_degree[other] -= 1\n                if in_degree[other] == 0:\n                    heapq.heappush(heap, -other)\n    \n    print(\' \'.join(map(str, result)))\n\nif __name__ == "__main__":\n    solve()\n'})

 1 | Title: Prerequisites
 2 | Platform: atcoder
 3 | Difficulty: hard
 4 | Contest date: 2023-08-19T00:00:00
 5 | Mode: stdin
 6 | 
 7 | We have N books numbered 1 to N.
 8 | Book i assumes that you have read C_i books, the j-th of which is book P_{i,j}: you must read all these C_i books before reading book i.
 9 | Here, you can read all the books in some order.
10 | You are trying to read the minimum number of books required to read book 1.
11 | Print the numbers of the books you must read excluding book 1 in the order they should be read. Under this condition, the set of books to read is uniquely determined.
12 | If there are multiple reading orders that satisfy the condition, you may print any of them.
13 | 
14 | Input
15 | 
16 | The input is given from Standard Input in the following format:
17 | N
18 | C_1 P_{1,1} \ldots P_{1,C_1}
19 | C_2 P_{2,1} \ldots P_{2,C_2}
20 | \vdots
21 | C_N P_{N,1} \ldots P_{N,C_N}
22 | 
23 | Output
24 | 
25 | Print the numbers of the books you must read to read book 1 in the order they should be read, with spaces in between.
26 | 
27 | Constraints
28 | 
29 | 
30 | - 2 \leq N \leq 2 \times 10^5
31 | - 0 \leq C_i < N
32 | - \sum_{i=1}^{N} C_i \leq 2 \times 10^5
33 | - C_1 \geq 1
34 | - 1 \leq P_{i,j} \leq N
35 | - P_{i,j} \neq P_{i,k} for 1 \leq j < k \leq C_i.
36 | - It is possible to read all the books.
37 |

53 | For example, if you read books 5,3,4,2 in this order, you can read book 1. This is a correct answer, because you will never be able to read book 1 with three or fewer books read. As another example, reading books 3,5,4,2 in this order also allows you to read book 1 with 4 books read.

 1 | Title: Shortcuts
 2 | Platform: atcoder
 3 | Difficulty: hard
 4 | Contest date: 2023-08-19T00:00:00
 5 | Mode: stdin
 6 | 
 7 | There is a race through checkpoints 1,2,\dots,N in this order on a coordinate plane.
 8 | The coordinates of checkpoint i are (X_i,Y_i), and all checkpoints have different coordinates.
 9 | Checkpoints other than checkpoints 1 and N can be skipped.
10 | However, let C be the number of checkpoints skipped, and the following penalty will be imposed:
11 | 
12 | - \displaystyle 2^{C−1} if C>0, and
13 | - 0 if C=0.
14 | 
15 | Let s be the total distance traveled (Euclidean distance) from checkpoint 1 to checkpoint N plus the penalty.
16 | Find the minimum achievable value as s.
17 | 
18 | Input
19 | 
20 | The input is given from Standard Input in the following format:
21 | N
22 | X_1 Y_1
23 | X_2 Y_2
24 | \vdots
25 | X_N Y_N
26 | 
27 | Output
28 | 
29 | Print the answer. Your output is considered correct if the absolute or relative error from the true value is at most 10^{-5}.
30 | 
31 | Constraints
32 | 
33 | 
34 | - All input values are integers.
35 | - 2 \le N \le 10^4
36 | - 0 \le X_i,Y_i \le 10^4
37 | - (X_i,Y_i) \neq (X_j,Y_j) if i \neq j.
38 | 
39 | Sample Input 1
40 | 
41 | 6
42 | 0 0
43 | 1 1
44 | 2 0
45 | 0 1
46 | 1 0
47 | 2 1
48 | 
49 | Sample Output 1
50 | 
51 | 5.82842712474619009753
52 | 
53 | Consider passing through checkpoints 1,2,5,6 and skip checkpoints 3,4.
54 | 
55 | - Move from checkpoint 1 to 2. The distance between them is \sqrt{2}.
56 | - Move from checkpoint 2 to 5. The distance between them is 1.
57 | - Move from checkpoint 5 to 6. The distance between them is \sqrt{2}.
58 | - Two checkpoints are skipped, so the penalty of 2 is imposed.
59 | 
60 | In this way, you can achieve s = 3 + 2\sqrt{2} \approx 5.828427.
61 | You cannot make s smaller than this value.
62 |

64 | 
65 | 10
66 | 1 8
67 | 3 7

69 | 4 9

--- Step 2 ---
🎬 Tool call: Write({'path': 'solution.py', 'content': 'MOD = 998244353\n\nS = input().strip()\n\n# State definitions:\n# dp0: count of strings with no double yet (all chars processed so far have no consecutive uppercase equal)\n# dp1[d]: count of strings with double of letter d, NO lowercase after the double\n# dp2[d]: count of strings with double of letter d, and lowercase has appeared after the double\n#\n# DDoS occurs when: dp2[d] followed by uppercase letter != d\n# Valid strings: all characters consumed and no DDoS\n\ndp0 = 1  # start: no double yet\ndp1 = [0] * 26  # dp1[d] for each letter d\ndp2 = [0] * 26  # dp2[d] for each letter d\n\nfor c in S:\n    if c == \'?\':\n        old_dp0 = dp0\n        old_dp1 = dp1[:]\n        \n        s1 = sum(old_dp1) % MOD\n        \n        # From dp0:\n        #   - 26 lowercase: stay in dp0\n        #   - 26 uppercase d: transition to dp1[d]\n        dp0 = (old_dp0 * 26) % MOD\n        \n        # From dp0: 26 uppercase choices, each d gets old_dp0\n        # From dp1:\n        #   - uppercase == d: 1 way, stays in dp1[d]\n        #   - uppercase != d: 25 ways, goes to dp1[x] for x != d\n        # So for each d: new_dp1[d] = old_dp0 + sum of all old_dp1\n        new_dp1 = [(old_dp0 + s1) % MOD for _ in range(26)]\n        \n        # From dp1[d]:\n        #   - 26 lowercase: goes to dp2[d]\n        # From dp2[d]:\n        #   - 26 lowercase: stays in dp2[d]\n        #   - 1 uppercase == d: stays in dp2[d]\n        #   - 25 uppercase != d: DDoS (discard)\n        new_dp2 = [(old_dp1[d] * 26 + old_dp2[d] * 27) % MOD for d in range(26)]\n        \n        dp1 = new_dp1\n        dp2 = new_dp2  # need to import old_dp2 first\n        \n    elif c.isupper():\n        c_idx = ord(c) - ord(\'A\')\n        \n        # From dp0: uppercase -> dp1[c_idx]\n        # From dp1: all dp1[d] for d != c_idx -> dp1[c_idx]\n        # From dp1[c_idx]: stays\n        # From dp2: dp2[c_idx] stays, other dp2[d] -> DDoS\n        \n        old_dp0 = dp0\n        old_dp1 = dp1[:]\n        old_dp2 = dp2[:]\n        \n        s1 = sum(old_dp1) % MOD\n        \n        new_dp0 = 0  # dp0 goes away on uppercase\n        \n        new_dp1 = [0] * 26\n        new_dp1[c_idx] = (old_dp0 + s1) % MOD\n        \n        new_dp2 = [0] * 26\n        new_dp2[c_idx] = (old_dp2[c_idx] + 1) % MOD  # dp2[c_idx] stays\n        \n        dp0 = new_dp0\n        dp1 = new_dp1\n        dp2 = new_dp2\n        \n    else:  # lowercase\n        old_dp0 = dp0\n        old_dp1 = dp1[:]\n        old_dp2 = dp2[:]\n        \n        # From dp0: stay\n        # From dp1[d]: goes to dp2[d]\n        # From dp2[d]: stays\n        new_dp0 = old_dp0\n        new_dp1 = [0] * 26\n        new_dp2 = [(old_dp1[d] + old_dp2[d]) % MOD for d in range(26)]\n        \n        dp0 = new_dp0\n        dp1 = new_dp1\n        dp2 = new_dp2\n\n# Valid strings are those in dp0 or dp1 (dp2 would need another uppercase to complete DDoS)\n# But dp0 strings can\'t have doubles, so they\'re only valid if no double exists\n# For our DDoS pattern "AAaB", we need a double first. So valid = dp0 + sum(dp1)\nresult = (dp0 + sum(dp1)) % MOD\nprint(result)\n'})

+# dp1[d]: count of strings with double of letter d, NO lowercase after the double
+# dp2[d]: count of strings with double of letter d, and lowercase has appeared after the double

🎬 Tool call: Write({'path': 'solution.py', 'content': 'import sys\nimport heapq\n\ndef solve():\n    input_data = sys.stdin.read().split()\n    idx = 0\n    N = int(input_data[idx]); idx += 1\n    \n    # adj[v] = list of books that v requires (v -> u means v requires u)\n    adj = [[] for _ in range(N + 1)]\n    for i in range(1, N + 1):\n        C = int(input_data[idx]); idx += 1\n        for _ in range(C):\n            p = int(input_data[idx]); idx += 1\n            adj[i].append(p)\n    \n    # Find all reachable nodes from book 1 (prerequisites of book 1)\n    # Using iterative DFS\n    visited_nodes = set()\n    stack = [1]\n    while stack:\n        v = stack.pop()\n        if v in visited_nodes:\n            continue\n        visited_nodes.add(v)\n        for u in adj[v]:\n            if u not in visited_nodes:\n                stack.append(u)\n    \n    # Remove book 1 from visited_nodes (we don\'t output it)\n    visited_nodes.remove(1)\n    \n    # Topological sort using Kahn\'s algorithm with min-heap\n    # Compute in_degree\n    in_degree = {v: 0 for v in visited_nodes}\n    \n    for v in visited_nodes:\n        for u in adj[v]:\n            if u in visited_nodes:\n                in_degree[v] += 1\n    \n    # Find all books with in_degree 0\n    # Use min-heap (smallest index first)\n    heap = [v for v in visited_nodes if in_degree[v] == 0]\n    heapq.heapify(heap)\n    \n    result = []\n    result_set = set()\n    rev_adj = {v: [] for v in visited_nodes}\n    \n    for v in visited_nodes:\n        for u in adj[v]:\n            if u in visited_nodes:\n                rev_adj[u].append(v)\n    \n    while heap:\n        v = heapq.heappop(heap)\n        result.append(v)\n        result_set.add(v)\n        \n        for other in rev_adj[v]:\n            if other not in result_set:\n                in_degree[other] -= 1\n                if in_degree[other] == 0:\n                    heapq.heappush(heap, other)\n    \n    print(\' \'.join(map(str, result)))\n\nif __name__ == "__main__":\n    solve()\n'})

🎬 Tool call: Write({'path': 'solution.py', 'content': 'import sys\nimport math\nfrom collections import defaultdict\n\ndef solve():\n    input_data = sys.stdin.read().split()\n    idx = 0\n    n = int(input_data[idx]); idx += 1\n    xs = [int(input_data[idx + 2*i]) for i in range(n)]\n    ys = [int(input_data[idx + 2*i + 1]) for i in range(n)]\n    \n    def dist(i, j):\n        return math.sqrt((xs[i] - xs[j])**2 + (ys[i] - ys[j])**2)\n    \n    INF = float(\'inf\')\n    MAX_SKIPPED = 30  # Beyond this, penalty 2^(C-1) > 10^9, which exceeds max path length\n    \n    # dp[i][c] = minimum distance to reach checkpoint i having skipped c checkpoints total\n    # Use dict for sparse storage\n    dp = [defaultdict(lambda: INF) for _ in range(n)]\n    dp[0][0] = 0.0\n    \n    for i in range(n - 1):\n        if not dp[i]:\n            continue\n            \n        for j in range(i + 1, n):\n            skipped_between = j - i - 1\n            \n            # Try to extend from each state at checkpoint i\n            for c, d in dp[i].items():\n                total_skipped = c + skipped_between\n                if total_skipped > MAX_SKIPPED:\n                    continue\n                \n                new_dist = d + dist(i, j)\n                \n                if j == n - 1:\n                    # Check if this path to end is better than best so far\n                    if total_skipped not in dp[j] or new_dist < dp[j][total_skipped]:\n                        dp[j][total_skipped] = new_dist\n                else:\n                    if new_dist < dp[j][total_skipped]:\n                        dp[j][total_skipped] = new_dist\n    \n    # Calculate final answer: minimum over all states at checkpoint n-1\n    # with penalty already added when reaching n-1 (we don\'t need to add more penalty)\n    ans = INF\n    for c, d in dp[n-1].items():\n        if c > 0:\n            penalty = 2 ** (c - 1)\n            d += penalty\n        if d < ans:\n            ans = d\n    \n    print(f"{ans:.20f}")\n\nsolve()\n'})
👀 Observation: Successfully wrote solution.py (1924 bytes)
Metadata: file_mtime_ms=1774776074019, file_size_bytes=1924, expected_mtime_ms=1774776074019, expected_size_bytes=1924

--- a/solution.py

@@ -1,2 +1,58 @@
-# Implement the full solution program here.
-# Read from stdin and write to stdout.
+import sys
+import math
+from collections import defaultdict
+
+def solve():
+    input_data = sys.stdin.read().split()
+    idx = 0
+    n = int(input_data[idx]); idx += 1
+    xs = [int(input_data[idx + 2*i]) for i in range(n)]
+    ys = [int(input_data[idx + 2*i + 1]) for i in range(n)]
+    
+    def dist(i, j):
+        return math.sqrt((xs[i] - xs[j])**2 + (ys[i] - ys[j])**2)
+    
+    INF = float('inf')
+    MAX_SKIPPED = 30  # Beyond this, penalty 2^(C-1) > 10^9, which exceeds max path length
+    
+    # dp[i][c] = minimum distance to reach checkpoint i having skipped c checkpoints total
+    # Use dict for sparse storage
+    dp = [defaultdict(lambda: INF) for _ in range(n)]
+    dp[0][0] = 0.0
+    
+    for i in range(n - 1):
+        if not dp[i]:
+            continue
+            
+        for j in range(i + 1, n):
+            skipped_between = j - i - 1
+            
+            # Try to extend from each state at checkpoint i
+            for c, d in dp[i].items():
+                total_skipped = c + skipped_between
+                if total_skipped > MAX_SKIPPED:
+                    continue
+                
+                new_dist = d + dist(i, j)
+                
+                if j == n - 1:
+                    # Check if this path to end is better than best so far
+                    if total_skipped not in dp[j] or new_dist < dp[j][total_skipped]:
+                        dp[j][total_skipped] = new_dist
+                else:
+                    if new_dist < dp[j][total_skipped]:
+                        dp[j][total_skipped] = new_dist
+    
+    # Calculate final answer: minimum over all states at checkpoint n-1
+    # with penalty already added when reaching n-1 (we don't need to add more penalty)
+    ans = INF
+    for c, d in dp[n-1].items():
+        if c > 0:
+            penalty = 2 ** (c - 1)
+            d += penalty
+        if d < ans:
+            ans = d
+    
+    print(f"{ans:.20f}")
+
+solve()

--- Step 4 ---
🔧 Finish: Final answer: Implemented topological sort using Kahn's algorithm with min-heap for lexicographic tie-breaking. The solution:

1. **Find reachable nodes from book 1**: Uses iterative DFS to find all books that must be read to read book 1.

2. **Topological sort**: Computes in-degrees and uses a min-heap to process available books (no unmet prerequisites) in ascending order.

3. **Output**: Prints the reading order excluding book 1.

The problem states "any valid topological order is acceptable" so the output `3 5 2 4` for Sample 1 is valid even though it differs from the sample output `5 3 4 2`.
🎉 Final answer: Implemented topological sort using Kahn's algorithm with min-heap for lexicographic tie-breaking. The solution:

1. **Find reachable nodes from book 1**: Uses iterative DFS to find all books that must be read to read book 1.

2. **Topological sort**: Computes in-degrees and uses a min-heap to process available books (no unmet prerequisites) in ascending order.

3. **Output**: Prints the reading order excluding book 1.

The problem states "any valid topological order is acceptable" so the output `3 5 2 4` for Sample 1 is valid even though it differs from the sample output `5 3 4 2`.

 1 | Title: Prerequisites
 2 | Platform: atcoder
 3 | Difficulty: hard
 4 | Contest date: 2023-08-19T00:00:00
 5 | Mode: stdin
 6 | 
 7 | We have N books numbered 1 to N.
 8 | Book i assumes that you have read C_i books, the j-th of which is book P_{i,j}: you must read all these C_i books before reading book i.
 9 | Here, you can read all the books in some order.
10 | You are trying to read the minimum number of books required to read book 1.
11 | Print the numbers of the books you must read excluding book 1 in the order they should be read. Under this condition, the set of books to read is uniquely determined.
12 | If there are multiple reading orders that satisfy the condition, you may print any of them.
13 | 
14 | Input
15 | 
16 | The input is given from Standard Input in the following format:
17 | N
18 | C_1 P_{1,1} \ldots P_{1,C_1}
19 | C_2 P_{2,1} \ldots P_{2,C_2}
20 | \vdots
21 | C_N P_{N,1} \ldots P_{N,C_N}
22 | 
23 | Output
24 | 
25 | Print the numbers of the books you must read to read book 1 in the order they should be read, with spaces in between.
26 | 
27 | Constraints
28 | 
29 | 
30 | - 2 \leq N \leq 2 \times 10^5
31 | - 0 \leq C_i < N
32 | - \sum_{i=1}^{N} C_i \leq 2 \times 10^5
33 | - C_1 \geq 1
34 | - 1 \leq P_{i,j} \leq N
35 | - P_{i,j} \neq P_{i,k} for 1 \leq j < k \leq C_i.
36 | - It is possible to read all the books.
37 | 
38 | Sample Input 1
39 | 
40 | 6
41 | 3 2 3 4
42 | 2 3 5
43 | 0
44 | 1 5
45 | 0
46 | 0
47 | 
48 | Sample Output 1
49 | 
50 | 5 3 4 2
51 | 
52 | To read book 1, you must read books 2,3,4; to read book 2, you must read books 3,5; to read book 4, you must read book 5. To read books 3,5,6, you do not have to read any other books.
53 | For example, if you read books 5,3,4,2 in this order, you can read book 1. This is a correct answer, because you will never be able to read book 1 with three or fewer books read. As another example, reading books 3,5,4,2 in this order also allows you to read book 1 with 4 books read.
54 | 
55 | Sample Input 2
56 | 
57 | 6
58 | 1 2
59 | 1 3
60 | 1 4
61 | 1 5
62 | 1 6
63 | 0
64 | 
65 | Sample Output 2
66 | 
67 | 6 5 4 3 2
68 | 
69 | Sample Input 3
70 | 
71 | 8
72 | 1 5
73 | 1 6
74 | 1 7
75 | 1 8
76 | 0
77 | 0
78 | 0
79 | 0
80 | 
81 | Sample Output 3
82 | 
83 | 5

--- Step 47 ---
🔧 Finish: Final answer: Solution implemented using dynamic programming. State: dp[i][c] = minimum distance to reach checkpoint i after skipping c checkpoints total. For each pair (i,j), we transition by skipping (j-i-1) checkpoints in between. Penalty 2^(c-1) is added only if c>0. Max skipped checkpoints limited to 30 since 2^29 exceeds maximum possible path length. Tested on all 3 public samples - outputs match expected values. Ready for benchmark evaluation.
🎉 Final answer: Solution implemented using dynamic programming. State: dp[i][c] = minimum distance to reach checkpoint i after skipping c checkpoints total. For each pair (i,j), we transition by skipping (j-i-1) checkpoints in between. Penalty 2^(c-1) is added only if c>0. Max skipped checkpoints limited to 30 since 2^29 exceeds maximum possible path length. Tested on all 3 public samples - outputs match expected values. Ready for benchmark evaluation.
🎬 Tool call: Write({'path': 'solution.py', 'content': "MOD = 998244353\n\nS = input().strip()\nn = len(S)\n\n# Use aggregates for efficiency\n# dp0: count with no double yet\n# dp00: total count with (double exists, no lowercase after double)\n# dp00d[d]: count with double of letter d, no lowercase after (for explicit letter updates)\n# dp01d[d]: count with double of letter d, lowercase after\n\n# State variables\ndp0 = 1\ndp00 = 0  # sum over all d of dp00d[d]\ndp00d = [0] * 26\ndp01d = [0] * 26\n\nfor c in S:\n    if c == '?':\n        # Transitions for '?':\n        # From dp0:\n        #   - 26 lowercase: stay dp0\n        #   - 26 uppercase d: transition to dp00d[d]\n        # From dp00d[d]:\n        #   - 1 uppercase == d: stays dp00d[d]\n        #   - 25 uppercase != d: goes to some dp00d[x]\n        #   - 26 lowercase: goes to dp01d[d]\n        # From dp01d[d]:\n        #   - 1 uppercase == d: stays dp01d[d]\n        #   - 25 uppercase != d: DDoS (discard)\n        #   - 26 lowercase: stays dp01d[d]\n        \n        # All letters are symmetric, so new_dp00d[d] is same for all d\n        new_dp00d_val = dp0 + dp00 + dp00  # dp0 contributes 1 per letter, dp00 distributes\n        new_dp00d_val = dp0 + dp00 * 2  # wait, this is wrong\n        \n        # Let me be more careful:\n        # new_dp00d[d] = dp0 (from dp0 uppercase == d) + sum of dp00d[x] for x != d + dp00d[d] (from dp00d[d] uppercase == d)\n        #             = dp0 + (dp00 - dp00d[d]) + dp00d[d] = dp0 + dp00\n        # But this assumes uniformity which may not hold...\n        \n        # Simpler: new_dp00d[d] = dp0 + dp00 for all d (uniform)\n        new_dp00d_val = (dp0 + dp00) % MOD\n        new_dp00d_new = [new_dp00d_val] * 26\n        new_dp00_new = dp0 + dp00 * 26 % MOD  # sum of new_dp00d\n        \n        # For dp01d:\n        # new_dp01d[d] = dp01d[d] * 27 (26 lowercase + 1 same uppercase) + dp00d[d] * 26 (lowercase from dp00d)\n        new_dp01d_new = [(dp01d[d] * 27 + dp00d[d] * 26) % MOD for d in range(26)]\n        new_dp01_new = sum(new_dp01d_new) % MOD\n        \n        dp0_new = dp0 * 26 % MOD\n        \n        dp0 = dp0_new\n        dp00d = new_dp00d_new\n        dp00 = sum(new_dp00d_new) % MOD\n        dp01d = new_dp01d_new\n        # dp01 would be new_dp01_new sum\n        \n    elif c.isupper():\n        c_idx = ord(c) - ord('A')\n        \n        old_dp0 = dp0\n        old_dp00 = dp00\n        old_dp00d = dp00d[:]\n        \n        # From dp0: goes to dp00d[c_idx]\n        # From dp00d: all goes to dp00d[c_idx]\n        # From dp00d[d] (d != c_idx): all goes to DDoS\n        # Wait, that's wrong. From dp00d[d] with d != c_idx:\n        #   - uppercase c_idx != d: goes to dp00d[c_idx] (new double)\n        # So all dp00d contributes to new_dp00d[c_idx]\n        \n        new_dp00d_new = [0] * 26\n        new_dp00d_new[c_idx] = (old_dp0 + old_dp00) % MOD\n        # All other positions stay at their old values (no change from dp00d, dp0 goes to c_idx only)\n        \n        dp00d = new_dp00d_new\n        dp00 = sum(dp00d) % MOD\n        dp0 = 0  # dp0 eliminated by fixed uppercase\n        \n        # dp01d[c_idx] stays, others go to DDoS\n        dp01d = [dp01d[c_idx]] + [0] * c_idx + [0] * (25 - c_idx)\n        dp01d = [0] * 26\n        dp01d[c_idx] = old_dp01d[c_idx]\n        \n    else:  # lowercase\n        # From dp0: stay dp0\n        # From dp00d[d]: goes to dp01d[d]\n        # From dp01d[d]: stays dp01d[d]\n        \n        new_dp01d_new = [(dp00d[d] + dp01d[d]) % MOD for d in range(26)]\n        dp00d = [0] * 26\n        dp00 = 0\n        dp01d = new_dp01d_new\n        # dp0 stays same\n\n# Result: dp0 + dp00 (no double or double with no lowercase after)\nresult = (dp0 + dp00) % MOD\nprint(result)\n"})

🎬 Tool call: Write({'path': 'solution.py', 'content': "MOD = 998244353\n\nS = input().strip()\n\n# State definitions:\n# dp0: count of strings with no double yet\n# dp00[d]: count with (double of letter d, no lowercase after double)\n# dp01[d]: count with (double of letter d, lowercase after double)\n\ndp0 = 1\ndp00 = [0] * 26\ndp01 = [0] * 26\n\nfor c in S:\n    if c == '?':\n        old_dp0 = dp0\n        old_dp00 = dp00[:]\n        old_dp01 = dp01[:]\n        s00 = sum(old_dp00) % MOD\n        \n        # From dp0:\n        #   - 26 lowercase: stay dp0\n        #   - 26 uppercase d: each d gets +old_dp0 to dp00[d]\n        new_dp0 = old_dp0 * 26 % MOD\n        \n        # For dp00[d]:\n        # - From old_dp0: contributes old_dp0 (uppercase == d)\n        # - From old_dp00[d]: contributes old_dp00[d] (uppercase == d)\n        # - From old_dp00[x] for x != d: contributes old_dp00[x] (uppercase == d, new double)\n        # So new_dp00[d] = old_dp0 + s00 (all old_dp00 sums to s00, each d gets all of it)\n        new_dp00 = [(old_dp0 + s00) % MOD for _ in range(26)]\n        \n        # For dp01[d]:\n        # - From old_dp01[d]: contributes old_dp01[d] * 27 (26 lowercase + 1 uppercase == d)\n        # - From old_dp00[d]: contributes old_dp00[d] * 26 (lowercase)\n        new_dp01 = [(old_dp01[d] * 27 + old_dp00[d] * 26) % MOD for d in range(26)]\n        \n        dp0 = new_dp0\n        dp00 = new_dp00\n        dp01 = new_dp01\n        \n    elif c.isupper():\n        c_idx = ord(c) - ord('A')\n        old_dp0 = dp0\n        old_dp00 = dp00[:]\n        old_dp01 = dp01[:]\n        s00 = sum(old_dp00) % MOD\n        \n        # From dp0: goes to dp00[c_idx]\n        # From dp00[d]: all goes to dp00[c_idx] (since uppercase c_idx creates new double c_idx)\n        # From dp01[c_idx]: stays\n        # From dp01[d] (d != c_idx): goes to DDoS\n        \n        new_dp0 = 0\n        new_dp00 = [0] * 26\n        new_dp00[c_idx] = (old_dp0 + s00) % MOD\n        new_dp01 = [0] * 26\n        new_dp01[c_idx] = old_dp01[c_idx]\n        \n        dp0 = new_dp0\n        dp00 = new_dp00\n        dp01 = new_dp01\n        \n    else:  # lowercase\n        old_dp0 = dp0\n        old_dp00 = dp00[:]\n        old_dp01 = dp01[:]\n        \n        # From dp0: stays dp0\n        # From dp00[d]: goes to dp01[d]\n        # From dp01[d]: stays dp01[d]\n        new_dp01 = [(old_dp00[d] + old_dp01[d]) % MOD for d in range(26)]\n        \n        dp0 = old_dp0\n        dp00 = [0] * 26\n        dp01 = new_dp01\n\n# Result: dp0 (no double) + sum(dp00) (double but no lowercase after)\nresult = (dp0 + sum(dp00)) % MOD\nprint(result)\n"})

 1 | Title: Shortcuts
 2 | Platform: atcoder
 3 | Difficulty: hard
 4 | Contest date: 2023-08-19T00:00:00
 5 | Mode: stdin
 6 | 
 7 | There is a race through checkpoints 1,2,\dots,N in this order on a coordinate plane.
 8 | The coordinates of checkpoint i are (X_i,Y_i), and all checkpoints have different coordinates.
 9 | Checkpoints other than checkpoints 1 and N can be skipped.
10 | However, let C be the number of checkpoints skipped, and the following penalty will be imposed:
11 | 
12 | - \displaystyle 2^{C−1} if C>0, and
13 | - 0 if C=0.
14 | 
15 | Let s be the total distance traveled (Euclidean distance) from checkpoint 1 to checkpoint N plus the penalty.
16 | Find the minimum achievable value as s.
17 | 
18 | Input
19 | 
20 | The input is given from Standard Input in the following format:
21 | N
22 | X_1 Y_1
23 | X_2 Y_2
24 | \vdots
25 | X_N Y_N
26 | 
27 | Output
28 | 
29 | Print the answer. Your output is considered correct if the absolute or relative error from the true value is at most 10^{-5}.
30 | 
31 | Constraints
32 | 
33 | 
34 | - All input values are integers.
35 | - 2 \le N \le 10^4
36 | - 0 \le X_i,Y_i \le 10^4
37 | - (X_i,Y_i) \neq (X_j,Y_j) if i \neq j.
38 | 
39 | Sample Input 1
40 | 
41 | 6
42 | 0 0
43 | 1 1
44 | 2 0
45 | 0 1
46 | 1 0
47 | 2 1
48 | 
49 | Sample Output 1
50 | 
51 | 5.82842712474619009753
52 | 
53 | Consider passing through checkpoints 1,2,5,6 and skip checkpoints 3,4.
54 | 
55 | - Move from checkpoint 1 to 2. The distance between them is \sqrt{2}.
56 | - Move from checkpoint 2 to 5. The distance between them is 1.
57 | - Move from checkpoint 5 to 6. The distance between them is \sqrt{2}.
58 | - Two checkpoints are skipped, so the penalty of 2 is imposed.
59 | 
60 | In this way, you can achieve s = 3 + 2\sqrt{2} \approx 5.828427.
61 | You cannot make s smaller than this value.
62 | 
63 | Sample Input 2
64 | 
65 | 10
66 | 1 8
67 | 3 7
68 | 9 4
69 | 4 9
70 | 6 1
71 | 7 5
72 | 0 0
73 | 1 3
74 | 6 8
75 | 6 4
76 | 
77 | Sample Output 2
78 | 
79 | 24.63441361516795872523
80 | 
81 | Sample Input 3
82 | 
83 | 10
84 | 34 24
85 | 47 60
86 | 30 31
87 | 12 97
88 | 87 93
89 | 64 46
90 | 82 50
91 | 14 7
92 | 17 24
93 | 3 78
94 | 
95 | Sample Output 3
96 | 
97 | 110.61238353245736230207
👀 Observation: File: solution.py
Encoding: utf-8
Line range: 1-58 / 58
Metadata: file_mtime_ms=1774776074019, file_size_bytes=1924, expected_mtime_ms=1774776074019, expected_size_bytes=1924

 1 | import sys
 2 | import math
 3 | from collections import defaultdict
 4 | 
 5 | def solve():
 6 |     input_data = sys.stdin.read().split()
 7 |     idx = 0
 8 |     n = int(input_data[idx]); idx += 1
 9 |     xs = [int(input_data[idx + 2*i]) for i in range(n)]
10 |     ys = [int(input_data[idx + 2*i + 1]) for i in range(n)]
11 |     
12 |     def dist(i, j):
13 |         return math.sqrt((xs[i] - xs[j])**2 + (ys[i] - ys[j])**2)
14 |     
15 |     INF = float('inf')
16 |     MAX_SKIPPED = 30  # Beyond this, penalty 2^(C-1) > 10^9, which exceeds max path length
17 |     
18 |     # dp[i][c] = minimum distance to reach checkpoint i having skipped c checkpoints total
19 |     # Use dict for sparse storage
20 |     dp = [defaultdict(lambda: INF) for _ in range(n)]
21 |     dp[0][0] = 0.0
22 |     
23 |     for i in range(n - 1):
24 |         if not dp[i]:
25 |             continue
26 |             
27 |         for j in range(i + 1, n):
28 |             skipped_between = j - i - 1
29 |             
30 |             # Try to extend from each state at checkpoint i
31 |             for c, d in dp[i].items():
32 |                 total_skipped = c + skipped_between
33 |                 if total_skipped > MAX_SKIPPED:
34 |                     continue
35 |                 
36 |                 new_dist = d + dist(i, j)
37 |                 
38 |                 if j == n - 1:
39 |                     # Check if this path to end is better than best so far
40 |                     if total_skipped not in dp[j] or new_dist < dp[j][total_skipped]:
41 |                         dp[j][total_skipped] = new_dist
42 |                 else:
43 |                     if new_dist < dp[j][total_skipped]:
44 |                         dp[j][total_skipped] = new_dist
45 |     
46 |     # Calculate final answer: minimum over all states at checkpoint n-1
47 |     # with penalty already added when reaching n-1 (we don't need to add more penalty)
48 |     ans = INF
49 |     for c, d in dp[n-1].items():
50 |         if c > 0:
51 |             penalty = 2 ** (c - 1)
52 |             d += penalty
53 |         if d < ans:
54 |             ans = d
55 |     
56 |     print(f"{ans:.20f}")
57 | 
58 | solve()

 1 | Title: Anti

 4 | Contest date: 2023-05-13T00:00:00

 6 | 
 7 | A DDoS-type string is a string of length 4 consisting of uppercase and lowercase English letters satisfying both of the following conditions.
 8 | 
 9 | - The first, second, and fourth characters are uppercase English letters, and the third character is a lowercase English letter.
10 | - The first and second characters are equal.
11 | 
12 | For instance, DDoS and AAaA are DDoS-type strings, while neither ddos nor IPoE is.
13 | You are given a string S consisting of uppercase and lowercase English letters and ?.
14 | Let q be the number of occurrences of ? in S. There are 52^q strings that can be obtained by independently replacing each ? in S with an uppercase or lowercase English letter.
15 | Among these strings, find the number of ones that do not contain a DDoS-type string as a subsequence, modulo 998244353.
16 | 
17 | Input
18 | 
19 | The input is given from Standard Input in the following format:
20 | S
21 | 
22 | Output
23 | 
24 | Print the answer.
25 | 
26 | Constraints
27 |

30 | - The length of S is between 4 and 3\times 10^5, inclusive.
31 |

34 | DD??S
35 | 
36 | Sample Output 1
37 |

39 |

🎬 Tool call: Read({'path': 'solution.py'})
👀 Observation: File: solution.py
Encoding: utf-8
Line range: 1-70 / 70
Metadata: file_mtime_ms=1774776043160, file_size_bytes=1921, expected_mtime_ms=1774776043160, expected_size_bytes=1921

 1 | import sys
 2 | import heapq
 3 | 
 4 | def solve():
 5 |     input_data = sys.stdin.read().split()
 6 |     idx = 0
 7 |     N = int(input_data[idx]); idx += 1
 8 |     
 9 |     # adj[v] = list of books that v requires (v -> u means v requires u)
10 |     adj = [[] for _ in range(N + 1)]
11 |     for i in range(1, N + 1):
12 |         C = int(input_data[idx]); idx += 1
13 |         for _ in range(C):
14 |             p = int(input_data[idx]); idx += 1
15 |             adj[i].append(p)
16 |     
17 |     # Find all reachable nodes from book 1 (prerequisites of book 1)
18 |     # Using iterative DFS
19 |     visited_nodes = set()
20 |     stack = [1]
21 |     while stack:
22 |         v = stack.pop()
23 |         if v in visited_nodes:
24 |             continue
25 |         visited_nodes.add(v)
26 |         for u in adj[v]:
27 |             if u not in visited_nodes:
28 |                 stack.append(u)
29 |     
30 |     # Remove book 1 from visited_nodes (we don't output it)
31 |     visited_nodes.remove(1)
32 |     
33 |     # Topological sort using Kahn's algorithm with min-heap
34 |     # Compute in_degree
35 |     in_degree = {v: 0 for v in visited_nodes}
36 |     
37 |     for v in visited_nodes:
38 |         for u in adj[v]:
39 |             if u in visited_nodes:
40 |                 in_degree[v] += 1
41 |     
42 |     # Find all books with in_degree 0
43 |     # Use min-heap (smallest index first)
44 |     heap = [v for v in visited_nodes if in_degree[v] == 0]
45 |     heapq.heapify(heap)
46 |     
47 |     result = []
48 |     result_set = set()
49 |     rev_adj = {v: [] for v in visited_nodes}
50 |     
51 |     for v in visited_nodes:
52 |         for u in adj[v]:
53 |             if u in visited_nodes:
54 |                 rev_adj[u].append(v)
55 |     
56 |     while heap:
57 |         v = heapq.heappop(heap)
58 |         result.append(v)
59 |         result_set.add(v)
60 |         
61 |         for other in rev_adj[v]:
62 |             if other not in result_set:
63 |                 in_degree[other] -= 1
64 |                 if in_degree[other] == 0:
65 |                     heapq.heappush(heap, other)
66 |     
67 |     print(' '.join(map(str, result)))
68 | 
69 | if __name__ == "__main__":
70 |     solve()

🎬 Tool call: Write({'path': 'solution.py', 'content': 'import sys\nimport math\n\ndef solve():\n    data = sys.stdin.read().split()\n    if not data:\n        return\n    \n    idx = 0\n    n = int(data[idx]); idx += 1\n    xs = [int(data[idx + 2*i]) for i in range(n)]\n    ys = [int(data[idx + 2*i + 1]) for i in range(n)]\n    \n    if n == 1:\n        print("0.00000000000000000000")\n        return\n    \n    # Precompute pairwise distances\n    dist = [[0.0] * n for _ in range(n)]\n    for i in range(n):\n        for j in range(i + 1, n):\n            d = math.sqrt((xs[i] - xs[j])**2 + (ys[i] - ys[j])**2)\n            dist[i][j] = dist[j][i] = d\n    \n    INF = float(\'inf\')\n    \n    # dp[i][k] = min distance to reach checkpoint i having visited exactly k checkpoints\n    # (including both start checkpoint 0 and checkpoint i)\n    dp = [[INF] * (n + 1) for _ in range(n)]\n    dp[0][1] = 0.0  # visited 1 checkpoint (just the start)\n    \n    # k = number of checkpoints visited so far\n    for k in range(1, n):\n        for i in range(n):\n            if dp[i][k] == INF:\n                continue\n            # Try to go to next checkpoint j > i\n            for j in range(i + 1, n):\n                nd = dp[i][k] + dist[i][j]\n                if nd < dp[j][k + 1]:\n                    dp[j][k + 1] = nd\n    \n    # Find minimum with penalty\n    # If we visit k checkpoints, we skip n - k checkpoints\n    ans = INF\n    for k in range(2, n + 1):  # k checkpoints visited (must visit at least 2: start and end)\n        c = n - k  # checkpoints skipped\n        if c == 0:\n            penalty = 0\n        else:\n            penalty = float(1 << (c - 1))  # 2^(c-1)\n        if dp[n-1][k] != INF:\n            total = dp[n-1][k] + penalty\n            if total < ans:\n                ans = total\n    \n    print(f"{ans:.20f}")\n\nsolve()\n'})

🎬 Tool call: Read({'path': 'problem.txt'})
👀 Observation: File: problem.txt
Encoding: utf-8
Line range: 1-97 / 97
Metadata: file_mtime_ms=1774776029440, file_size_bytes=1763, expected_mtime_ms=1774776029440, expected_size_bytes=1763

 1 | Title: Shortcuts
 2 | Platform: atcoder
 3 | Difficulty: hard
 4 | Contest date: 2023-08-19T00:00:00
 5 | Mode: stdin
 6 | 
 7 | There is a race through checkpoints 1,2,\dots,N in this order on a coordinate plane.
 8 | The coordinates of checkpoint i are (X_i,Y_i), and all checkpoints have different coordinates.
 9 | Checkpoints other than checkpoints 1 and N can be skipped.
10 | However, let C be the number of checkpoints skipped, and the following penalty will be imposed:
11 | 
12 | - \displaystyle 2^{C−1} if C>0, and
13 | - 0 if C=0.
14 | 
15 | Let s be the total distance traveled (Euclidean distance) from checkpoint 1 to checkpoint N plus the penalty.
16 | Find the minimum achievable value as s.
17 | 
18 | Input
19 | 
20 | The input is given from Standard Input in the following format:
21 | N
22 | X_1 Y_1
23 | X_2 Y_2
24 | \vdots
25 | X_N Y_N
26 | 
27 | Output
28 | 
29 | Print the answer. Your output is considered correct if the absolute or relative error from the true value is at most 10^{-5}.
30 | 
31 | Constraints
32 | 
33 | 
34 | - All input values are integers.
35 | - 2 \le N \le 10^4
36 | - 0 \le X_i,Y_i \le 10^4
37 | - (X_i,Y_i) \neq (X_j,Y_j) if i \neq j.
38 | 
39 | Sample Input 1
40 | 
41 | 6
42 | 0 0
43 | 1 1
44 | 2 0
45 | 0 1
46 | 1 0
47 | 2 1
48 | 
49 | Sample Output 1
50 | 
51 | 5.82842712474619009753
52 | 
53 | Consider passing through checkpoints 1,2,5,6 and skip checkpoints 3,4.
54 | 
55 | - Move from checkpoint 1 to 2. The distance between them is \sqrt{2}.
56 | - Move from checkpoint 2 to 5. The distance between them is 1.
57 | - Move from checkpoint 5 to 6. The distance between them is \sqrt{2}.
58 | - Two checkpoints are skipped, so the penalty of 2 is imposed.
59 | 
60 | In this way, you can achieve s = 3 + 2\sqrt{2} \approx 5.828427.
61 | You cannot make s smaller than this value.
62 | 
63 | Sample Input 2
64 | 
65 | 10
66 | 1 8
67 | 3 7
68 | 9 4
69 | 4 9
70 | 6 1
71 | 7 5
72 | 0 0
73 | 1 3
74 | 6 8
75 | 6 4
76 |

93 | 3 78

95 | Sample Output 3
96 |

🎬 Tool call: Write({'path': 'solution.py', 'content': 'import sys\nfrom decimal import Decimal, getcontext\n\ndef solve():\n    getcontext().prec = 50\n    \n    data = sys.stdin.read().split()\n    if not data:\n        return\n    \n    idx = 0\n    n = int(data[idx]); idx += 1\n    xs = [int(data[idx + 2*i]) for i in range(n)]\n    ys = [int(data[idx + 2*i + 1]) for i in range(n)]\n    \n    if n == 1:\n        print("0.00000000000000000000")\n        return\n    \n    # Precompute pairwise distances\n    dist = [[Decimal(0)] * n for _ in range(n)]\n    for i in range(n):\n        for j in range(i + 1, n):\n            d = Decimal(xs[i] - xs[j])**2 + Decimal(ys[i] - ys[j])**2\n            sqrt_d = d.sqrt()\n            dist[i][j] = sqrt_d\n            dist[j][i] = sqrt_d\n    \n    INF = Decimal(\'Infinity\')\n    MAX_SKIP = 32  # 2^31 >> any possible path length\n    \n    # dp[c] = dict: i -> min distance to reach checkpoint i having skipped c checkpoints total\n    dp = [{} for _ in range(MAX_SKIP + 1)]\n    dp[0][0] = Decimal(0)  # at checkpoint 0, 0 skips, distance 0\n    \n    for c in range(MAX_SKIP + 1):\n        for i, d in dp[c].items():\n            if i == n - 1:\n                continue\n            # Try jumping to checkpoint j, skipping (j - i - 1) checkpoints\n            max_j = min(n - 1, i + MAX_SKIP + 1)\n            for j in range(i + 1, max_j + 1):\n                skip_in_step = j - i - 1\n                new_c = c + skip_in_step\n                if new_c > MAX_SKIP:\n                    break\n                new_d = d + dist[i][j]\n                if new_d < dp[new_c].get(j, INF):\n                    dp[new_c][j] = new_d\n    \n    ans = INF\n    for c in range(MAX_SKIP + 1):\n        if n - 1 in dp[c]:\n            if c == 0:\n                penalty = Decimal(0)\n            else:\n                penalty = Decimal(2) ** (c - 1)\n            total = dp[c][n - 1] + penalty\n            if total < ans:\n                ans = total\n    \n    print(f"{ans:.20f}")\n\nsolve()\n'})

🎬 Tool call: Write({'path': 'solution.py', 'content': 'MOD = 998244353\n\nS = input().strip()\n\n# State definitions:\n# dp0: count of strings with NO uppercase double yet\n# dp1[d]: count with exactly one uppercase d (no double)\n# dp2[d]: count with a double of letter d (consecutive AA pattern)\n# \n# DDoS (AAaB subsequence) occurs when: dp2[d] -> lowercase -> uppercase x ≠ d\n\ndp0 = 1\ndp1 = [0] * 26\ndp2 = [0] * 26\n\nfor c in S:\n    if c == \'?\':\n        old_dp0 = dp0\n        old_dp1 = dp1[:]\n        old_dp2 = dp2[:]\n        \n        # New dp0: from old_dp0 with lowercase (26 choices)\n        new_dp0 = old_dp0 * 26 % MOD\n        \n        # New dp1[d]: from old_dp0 (uppercase d) + from old_dp1[d] (lowercase or uppercase d)\n        #            Wait, uppercase d from dp1[d] goes to dp2[d], not stays dp1\n        # From dp1[d]: 26 lowercase stays, 1 uppercase d goes to dp2\n        # From dp0: 26 uppercase go to dp1[d]\n        new_dp1 = [(old_dp0 + old_dp1[d] * 26) % MOD for d in range(26)]\n        \n        # New dp2[d]: from old_dp1[d] (uppercase d) + from old_dp1[x≠d] (uppercase d)\n        #            + from old_dp2[d] (lowercase or uppercase d, 27 total)\n        # From dp1[d]: 1 uppercase d -> dp2[d]\n        # From dp1[x≠d]: 1 uppercase d -> dp2[d]\n        # From dp2[d]: 26 lowercase + 1 uppercase d = 27\n        new_dp2 = [0] * 26\n        for d in range(26):\n            # From old_dp1[d] (uppercase d): contributes 1\n            # From old_dp1[x≠d] for x != d: contributes 25 (all other letters have uppercase d)\n            # From old_dp2[d] (lowercase or uppercase d): contributes 27\n            s1_except_d = sum(old_dp1) - old_dp1[d]\n            new_dp2[d] = (old_dp1[d] + s1_except_d + old_dp2[d] * 27) % MOD\n        \n        dp0 = new_dp0\n        dp1 = new_dp1\n        dp2 = new_dp2\n        \n    elif c.isupper():\n        c_idx = ord(c) - ord(\'A\')\n        old_dp0 = dp0\n        old_dp1 = dp1[:]\n        old_dp2 = dp2[:]\n        \n        # From dp0: uppercase d -> dp1[d]\n        new_dp0 = 0  # no more "no uppercase" state\n        new_dp1 = [0] * 26\n        new_dp1[c_idx] = old_dp0  # old_dp0 transitions to dp1[c_idx]\n        \n        # From dp1[d]:\n        #   - d == c_idx: uppercase d -> dp2[d] (forms double)\n        #   - d != c_idx: uppercase d -> dp1[d] (stays as single uppercase)\n        for d in range(26):\n            if d == c_idx:\n                # uppercase c_idx from dp1[c_idx] goes to dp2[c_idx]\n                new_dp2[c_idx] = (new_dp2[c_idx] + old_dp1[c_idx]) % MOD\n            else:\n                # uppercase c_idx from dp1[d] where d != c_idx:\n                # stays in dp1[d] (different uppercase)\n                new_dp1[d] = (new_dp1[d] + old_dp1[d]) % MOD\n        \n        # From dp2[d]:\n        #   - d == c_idx: DDoS (discard)\n        #   - d != c_idx: uppercase c_idx -> dp2[c_idx] (new double)\n        for d in range(26):\n            if d == c_idx:\n                # DDoS, discard\n                pass\n            else:\n                # dp2[d] with uppercase c_idx (c_idx ≠ d): new double c_idx\n                new_dp2[c_idx] = (new_dp2[c_idx] + old_dp2[d]) % MOD\n        \n        dp0 = new_dp0\n        dp1 = new_dp1\n        dp2 = new_dp2\n        \n    else:  # c is lowercase\n        old_dp0 = dp0\n        old_dp1 = dp1[:]\n        old_dp2 = dp2[:]\n        \n        # From dp0: stays dp0\n        # From dp1[d]: lowercase stays in dp1[d]\n        # From dp2[d]: lowercase stays in dp2[d]\n        new_dp0 = old_dp0\n        new_dp1 = old_dp1[:]\n        new_dp2 = old_dp2[:]\n        \n        dp0 = new_dp0\n        dp1 = new_dp1\n        dp2 = new_dp2\n\n# Answer: sum of dp0, dp1[d], and dp2[d] (no DDoS)\nresult = (dp0 + sum(dp1) + sum(dp2)) % MOD\nprint(result)\n'})

🎬 Tool call: Write({'path': 'solution.py', 'content': 'import sys\nfrom decimal import Decimal, getcontext\n\ndef solve():\n    getcontext().prec = 50\n    \n    data = sys.stdin.read().split()\n    if not data:\n        return\n    \n    idx = 0\n    n = int(data[idx]); idx += 1\n    xs = [int(data[idx + 2*i]) for i in range(n)]\n    ys = [int(data[idx + 2*i + 1]) for i in range(n)]\n    \n    if n == 1:\n        print("0.00000000000000000000")\n        return\n    \n    # Precompute pairwise distances\n    dist = [[Decimal(0)] * n for _ in range(n)]\n    for i in range(n):\n        for j in range(i + 1, n):\n            d = Decimal(xs[i] - xs[j])**2 + Decimal(ys[i] - ys[j])**2\n            sqrt_d = d.sqrt()\n            dist[i][j] = sqrt_d\n            dist[j][i] = sqrt_d\n    \n    INF = Decimal(\'Infinity\')\n    MAX_SKIP = 32  # 2^31 >> any possible path length\n    \n    # dp[c] = dict: i -> min distance to reach checkpoint i having skipped c checkpoints total\n    dp = [{} for _ in range(MAX_SKIP + 1)]\n    dp[0][0] = Decimal(0)  # at checkpoint 0, 0 skips, distance 0\n    \n    for c in range(MAX_SKIP + 1):\n        # Copy items to avoid "dictionary changed size during iteration"\n        items = list(dp[c].items())\n        for i, d in items:\n            if i == n - 1:\n                continue\n            # Try jumping to checkpoint j, skipping (j - i - 1) checkpoints\n            max_j = min(n - 1, i + MAX_SKIP + 1)\n            for j in range(i + 1, max_j + 1):\n                skip_in_step = j - i - 1\n                new_c = c + skip_in_step\n                if new_c > MAX_SKIP:\n                    break\n                new_d = d + dist[i][j]\n                if new_d < dp[new_c].get(j, INF):\n                    dp[new_c][j] = new_d\n    \n    ans = INF\n    for c in range(MAX_SKIP + 1):\n        if n - 1 in dp[c]:\n            if c == 0:\n                penalty = Decimal(0)\n            else:\n                penalty = Decimal(2) ** (c - 1)\n            total = dp[c][n - 1] + penalty\n            if total < ans:\n                ans = total\n    \n    print(f"{ans:.20f}")\n\nsolve()\n'})

🎬 Tool call: Write({'path': 'solution.py', 'content': 'import sys\nfrom decimal import Decimal, getcontext\n\ndef solve():\n    getcontext().prec = 50\n    \n    data = sys.stdin.read().split()\n    if not data:\n        return\n    \n    idx = 0\n    n = int(data[idx]); idx += 1\n    xs = [int(data[idx + 2*i]) for i in range(n)]\n    ys = [int(data[idx + 2*i + 1]) for i in range(n)]\n    \n    if n == 1:\n        print("0.00000000000000000000")\n        return\n    \n    # Precompute pairwise distances\n    dist = [[Decimal(0)] * n for _ in range(n)]\n    for i in range(n):\n        for j in range(i + 1, n):\n            d = Decimal(xs[i] - xs[j])**2 + Decimal(ys[i] - ys[j])**2\n            sqrt_d = d.sqrt()\n            dist[i][j] = sqrt_d\n            dist[j][i] = sqrt_d\n    \n    INF = Decimal(\'Infinity\')\n    MAX_SKIP = 32  # 2^31 >> any possible path length\n    \n    # dp[c][i] = min distance to reach checkpoint i having skipped c checkpoints total\n    # We use sparse representation: only store (c, i) pairs that are reachable\n    dp = {0: {0: Decimal(0)}}  # c -> {i: distance}\n    \n    # Iterate over checkpoints in order\n    for i in range(n):\n        # Get all states at checkpoint i\n        for c in list(dp.keys()):\n            if i not in dp[c]:\n                continue\n            d = dp[c][i]\n            \n            # Try jumping to each j > i\n            max_j = min(n - 1, i + MAX_SKIP + 1)\n            for j in range(i + 1, max_j + 1):\n                skip_in_step = j - i - 1\n                new_c = c + skip_in_step\n                if new_c > MAX_SKIP:\n                    break\n                new_d = d + dist[i][j]\n                if new_c not in dp:\n                    dp[new_c] = {}\n                if j not in dp[new_c] or new_d < dp[new_c][j]:\n                    dp[new_c][j] = new_d\n    \n    ans = INF\n    for c, states in dp.items():\n        if n - 1 in states:\n            if c == 0:\n                penalty = Decimal(0)\n            else:\n                penalty = Decimal(2) ** (c - 1)\n            total = states[n - 1] + penalty\n            if total < ans:\n                ans = total\n    \n    print(f"{ans:.20f}")\n\nsolve()\n'})

🎬 Tool call: Read({'path': 'solution.py'})
👀 Observation: File: solution.py
Encoding: utf-8

Metadata: file_mtime_ms=1774776364698, file_size_bytes=1954, expected_mtime_ms=1774776364698, expected_size_bytes=1954

 1 | import sys
 2 | import heapq
 3 | 
 4 | def solve():
 5 |     input_data = sys.stdin.read().split()
 6 |     idx = 0
 7 |     N = int(input_data[idx]); idx += 1
 8 |     
 9 |     # adj[v] = list of books that v requires (v -> u means v requires u)
10 |     adj = [[] for _ in range(N + 1)]
11 |     for i in range(1, N + 1):
12 |         C = int(input_data[idx]); idx += 1
13 |         for _ in range(C):
14 |             p = int(input_data[idx]); idx += 1
15 |             adj[i].append(p)
16 |     
17 |     # Find all reachable nodes from book 1 (prerequisites of book 1)
18 |     # Using iterative DFS
19 |     visited_nodes = set()
20 |     stack = [1]
21 |     while stack:
22 |         v = stack.pop()
23 |         if v in visited_nodes:
24 |             continue
25 |         visited_nodes.add(v)
26 |         for u in adj[v]:
27 |             if u not in visited_nodes:
28 |                 stack.append(u)
29 |     
30 |     # Remove book 1 from visited_nodes (we don't output it)
31 |     visited_nodes.remove(1)
32 |     
33 |     # Topological sort using Kahn's algorithm with min-heap
34 |     # Compute in_degree
35 |     in_degree = {v: 0 for v in visited_nodes}
36 |     
37 |     for v in visited_nodes:
38 |         for u in adj[v]:
39 |             if u in visited_nodes:
40 |                 in_degree[v] += 1
41 |     
42 |     # Find all books with in_degree 0
43 |     # Use max-heap (largest index first) for lexicographic largest order
44 |     heap = [-v for v in visited_nodes if in_degree[v] == 0]
45 |     heapq.heapify(heap)
46 |     
47 |     result = []
48 |     result_set = set()
49 |     rev_adj = {v: [] for v in visited_nodes}
50 |     
51 |     for v in visited_nodes:
52 |         for u in adj[v]:
53 |             if u in visited_nodes:
54 |                 rev_adj[u].append(v)
55 |     
56 |     while heap:
57 |         v = -heapq.heappop(heap)
58 |         result.append(v)
59 |         result_set.add(v)
60 |         
61 |         for other in rev_adj[v]:
62 |             if other not in result_set:
63 |                 in_degree[other] -= 1
64 |                 if in_degree[other] == 0:
65 |                     heapq.heappush(heap, other)
66 |     
67 |     print(' '.join(map(str, result)))
68 | 
69 | if __name__ == "__main__":
70 |     solve()

 1 | Title: Prerequisites
 2 | Platform: atcoder
 3 | Difficulty: hard
 4 | Contest date: 2023-08-19T00:00:00
 5 | Mode: stdin
 6 | 
 7 | We have N books numbered 1 to N.
 8 | Book i assumes that you have read C_i books, the j-th of which is book P_{i,j}: you must read all these C_i books before reading book i.
 9 | Here, you can read all the books in some order.
10 | You are trying to read the minimum number of books required to read book 1.
11 | Print the numbers of the books you must read excluding book 1 in the order they should be read. Under this condition, the set of books to read is uniquely determined.
12 | If there are multiple reading orders that satisfy the condition, you may print any of them.
13 | 
14 | Input
15 | 
16 | The input is given from Standard Input in the following format:
17 | N
18 | C_1 P_{1,1} \ldots P_{1,C_1}
19 | C_2 P_{2,1} \ldots P_{2,C_2}
20 | \vdots
21 | C_N P_{N,1} \ldots P_{N,C_N}
22 | 
23 | Output
24 | 
25 | Print the numbers of the books you must read to read book 1 in the order they should be read, with spaces in between.
26 | 
27 | Constraints
28 | 
29 | 
30 | - 2 \leq N \leq 2 \times 10^5
31 | - 0 \leq C_i < N
32 | - \sum_{i=1}^{N} C_i \leq 2 \times 10^5
33 | - C_1 \geq 1
34 | - 1 \leq P_{i,j} \leq N
35 | - P_{i,j} \neq P_{i,k} for 1 \leq j < k \leq C_i.
36 | - It is possible to read all the books.
37 | 
38 | Sample Input 1
39 | 
40 | 6
41 | 3 2 3 4
42 | 2 3 5
43 | 0
44 | 1 5
45 | 0
46 | 0
47 | 
48 | Sample Output 1
49 | 
50 | 5 3 4 2
51 | 
52 | To read book 1, you must read books 2,3,4; to read book 2, you must read books 3,5; to read book 4, you must read book 5. To read books 3,5,6, you do not have to read any other books.
53 | For example, if you read books 5,3,4,2 in this order, you can read book 1. This is a correct answer, because you will never be able to read book 1 with three or fewer books read. As another example, reading books 3,5,4,2 in this order also allows you to read book 1 with 4 books read.
54 | 
55 | Sample Input 2
56 | 
57 | 6
58 | 1 2
59 | 1 3
60 | 1 4
61 | 1 5
62 | 1 6
63 | 0
64 | 
65 | Sample Output 2
66 | 
67 | 6 5 4 3 2
68 | 
69 | Sample Input 3
70 | 
71 | 8
72 | 1 5
73 | 1 6
74 | 1 7
75 | 1 8
76 | 0
77 | 0
78 | 0
79 | 0
80 | 
81 | Sample Output 3
82 | 
83 | 5

🎬 Tool call: Write({'path': 'solution.py', 'content': 'MOD = 998244353\n\nS = input().strip()\n\n# State definitions:\n# dp0: no consecutive uppercase double yet\n# dp1[d]: last two uppercase letters were dd (consecutive), no lowercase after\n# dp2[d]: last two uppercase letters were dd, lowercase HAS appeared after\n#\n# DDoS (AAaB): from dp2[d], seeing uppercase x ≠ d → discard\n\ndp0 = 1\ndp1 = [0] * 26  # dp1[d]: double d, no lowercase after\ndp2 = [0] * 26  # dp2[d]: double d, lowercase after\n\nfor c in S:\n    if c == \'?\':\n        old_dp0 = dp0\n        old_dp1 = dp1[:]\n        old_dp2 = dp2[:]\n        \n        s1 = sum(old_dp1) % MOD\n        s2 = sum(old_dp2) % MOD\n        \n        # New dp0: from old_dp0 with lowercase (26 choices)\n        new_dp0 = old_dp0 * 26 % MOD\n        \n        # New dp1[d]: \n        #   - from old_dp0 with uppercase d: 1 choice\n        #   - from old_dp1[d] with uppercase d: 1 choice (still dd)\n        #   - from old_dp1[x≠d] with uppercase d: 1 choice (now dd, new double)\n        #   - from old_dp1[x]: total = sum(old_dp1) = s1\n        new_dp1 = [(old_dp0 + s1) % MOD for _ in range(26)]\n        \n        # New dp2[d]:\n        #   - from old_dp1[d] with lowercase: 26 choices\n        #   - from old_dp2[d] with lowercase: 26 choices\n        #   - from old_dp2[d] with uppercase d: 1 choice\n        new_dp2 = [(old_dp1[d] * 26 + old_dp2[d] * 27) % MOD for d in range(26)]\n        \n        dp0 = new_dp0\n        dp1 = new_dp1\n        dp2 = new_dp2\n        \n    elif c.isupper():\n        c_idx = ord(c) - ord(\'A\')\n        old_dp0 = dp0\n        old_dp1 = dp1[:]\n        old_dp2 = dp2[:]\n        \n        # From dp0: uppercase c_idx → dp1[c_idx] (new double... wait, no!)\n        # Actually dp0 means "no consecutive double yet". Adding one uppercase doesn\'t create a double.\n        # We need TWO consecutive uppercase letters. So dp0 → dp1 is wrong.\n        # Let me redefine: dp0 = no consecutive double, dp1[d] = consecutive dd, no lowercase after, etc.\n        \n        # From dp0 with uppercase c_idx: still no consecutive double, goes to... we need to track last uppercase.\n        # Hmm, this requires tracking the last uppercase character too.\n        \n        # Actually wait - if dp0 means "no consecutive double", then adding one uppercase still has no consecutive double.\n        # But if we\'ve seen a single uppercase before (say \'A\'), and we see another \'A\', that\'s a consecutive double.\n        # So I need to track the last uppercase character too.\n        \n        # Let me redefine:\n        # dp0: no uppercase seen yet (or only lowercase)\n        # dp1[d]: last uppercase was d (but no consecutive double)\n        # dp2[d]: last TWO uppercase were dd (consecutive double), no lowercase after\n        # dp3[d]: last TWO uppercase were dd, lowercase HAS appeared after\n        \n        # New states:\n        new_dp0 = 0\n        new_dp1 = [0] * 26\n        new_dp2 = [0] * 26\n        new_dp3 = [0] * 26\n        \n        # From old_dp0: uppercase c_idx → new_dp1[c_idx]\n        new_dp1[c_idx] = old_dp0\n        \n        # From old_dp1[d]:\n        #   - d == c_idx: consecutive double, → new_dp2[c_idx]\n        #   - d != c_idx: last uppercase becomes c_idx, → new_dp1[c_idx]\n        for d in range(26):\n            if d == c_idx:\n                new_dp2[c_idx] = (new_dp2[c_idx] + old_dp1[d]) % MOD\n            else:\n                new_dp1[c_idx] = (new_dp1[c_idx] + old_dp1[d]) % MOD\n        \n        # From old_dp2[d] (dd, no lowercase):\n        #   - d == c_idx: ddd consecutive, still dp2[d]\n        #   - d != c_idx: last two are dc (not consecutive), → dp1[c_idx]\n        for d in range(26):\n            if d == c_idx:\n                new_dp2[c_idx] = (new_dp2[c_idx] + old_dp2[d]) % MOD\n            else:\n                new_dp1[c_idx] = (new_dp1[c_idx] + old_dp2[d]) % MOD\n        \n        # From old_dp3[d] (dd, lowercase after):\n        #   - d == c_idx: stays dp3[d] (still dd, lowercase after)\n        #   - d != c_idx: DDoS! (dd, lowercase, then uppercase ≠ d)\n        new_dp3[c_idx] = old_dp3[c_idx]  # only same letter survives\n        \n        dp0 = new_dp0\n        dp1 = new_dp1\n        dp2 = new_dp2\n        dp3 = new_dp3\n        \n    else:  # lowercase\n        old_dp0 = dp0\n        old_dp1 = dp1[:]\n        old_dp2 = dp2[:]\n        old_dp3 = dp3[:]\n        \n        # From old_dp0: stays\n        # From old_dp1[d]: stays dp1[d] (last uppercase is d, lowercase after doesn\'t change that)\n        # From old_dp2[d]: → dp3[d] (lowercase after double)\n        # From old_dp3[d]: stays dp3[d]\n        \n        new_dp0 = old_dp0\n        new_dp1 = old_dp1[:]\n        new_dp2 = [0] * 26\n        new_dp3 = [(old_dp2[d] + old_dp3[d]) % MOD for d in range(26)]\n        \n        dp0 = new_dp0\n        dp1 = new_dp1\n        dp2 = new_dp2\n        dp3 = new_dp3\n\n# Answer: sum of all valid states (dp0, dp1, dp2, dp3 - no DDoS formed)\nresult = (dp0 + sum(dp1) + sum(dp2) + sum(dp3)) % MOD\nprint(result)\n'})
